channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: International Outcry Over Detention of Gaza-Bound Flotilla Activists by Israel
item.description: On May 20, 2026, Israel's far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir posted a video showing Gaza-bound flotilla activists detained with their hands tied and kneeling on the deck of an Israel...

item.title: WHO warns Ebola outbreak serious in DRC and neighbouring countries, global risk low
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) don warn say the Ebola outbreak wey dey happen for Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) na serious matter for both national and regional level, even though global...

item.title: Canadian Academic Gad Saad Relocates to the U.S. Amid Safety Concerns and Threats
item.description: Gad Saad, a prominent Canadian academic and author, has announced his permanent move from Montreal to the United States, citing escalating personal safety concerns. Saad, whose book 'Suicidal Empathy'...

item.title: Lagos State recovers over ₦450 million for consumers, sanctions non-compliant businesses
item.description: Lagos State government, through the Lagos State Consumer Protection Agency (LASCOPA), don recover more than ₦450 million for consumers wey suffer from unfair trade practices. Commissioner for Commerce...

item.title: UK Inflation Falls to 2.8% in April Amid Energy Support, But Pressure from Global Events Could Push Prices Higher
item.description: UK inflation has fallen to 2.8% in the year to April, down from 3.3% in March, largely due to lower gas and electricity bills aided by government support and falling wholesale energy costs. Despite th...

item.title: Keating backs CGT changes while Minns calls for bigger income tax relief
item.description: Former Prime Minister Paul Keating has come out in strong support of Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese following criticism of the recent capital gains tax (CGT) reforms. Keati...

item.title: Bhopal Court Denies Second Autopsy Request in Noida Woman's Death Case
item.description: On Wednesday, a local court in Madhya Pradesh dismissed the petition filed by the family of a Noida woman who was found dead at her marital residence in Bhopal, requesting a second post-mortem examina...

item.title: Tanzania Financial Sector Shows Strong Growth Across Banks, Capital Markets, and Social Security in 2025
item.description: In 2025, Tanzania's financial system demonstrated strong stability and growth, according to the Bank of Tanzania Financial Stability Report. The banking sector led the expansion with total assets incr...

item.title: Karachi Court Sentences Cleric for Sexual Abuse of Two Madressah Students
item.description: KARACHI: In a significant judgement, a sessions court in Karachi has sentenced Qari Muhammad Anwar, a cleric and madressah teacher, to two concurrent 15-year jail terms for sexually abusing two of his...

item.title: Keeping African Capital at Home Can Close the Continent’s Financing Gap
item.description: Africa dey provide cheap money to rich countries but dey pay high interest to foreign investors, wey dey slow down its own development. For example, central banks for Africa dey keep reserve funds abr...

item.title: Kenya Proposes Sh32.24bn Budget for Sports Ahead of AFCON 2027
item.description: The State Department for Sports in Kenya is pushing for a bigger budget of Sh32.24 billion for the 2026/27 financial year, up from the Sh25.49 billion earlier set. This increase comes as the country r...

item.title: NDLEA Dismantles Nigerian-Mexican Meth Lab in Ogun, Arrests Multiple Suspects and Seizes ₦480 Billion Worth of Drugs
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) don successfully take down one of the biggest methamphetamine labs wey dem ever discover for Nigeria. The operation happen for Ijebu East, Ogun State, ...

item.title: Five villagers wounded in Picong, Lanao del Sur due to clan conflict
item.description: On May 19, 2026, five residents of Barangay Pindolonan in Picong town, Lanao del Sur were wounded in a violent attack by armed assailants linked to a local clan dispute. The victims include Nassir Mar...

item.title: US Mint confirms Trump commemorative gold coins will not be issued by 250th Independence Day
item.description: The United States Mint has confirmed that commemorative gold coins featuring former President Donald Trump will not be ready in time for the nation’s 250th birthday celebrations on 4 July 2026. Accord...

item.title: How 1920s Women Embraced Cars as Symbols of Independence
item.description: In the 1920s, societal norms underwent dramatic changes, and the automobile emerged as a powerful symbol of freedom for women. No longer restricted to domestic roles or dependent on male drivers, wome...

item.title: PTI agrees to government dialogue if Imran Khan is freed
item.description: On Wednesday, Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) expressed readiness to engage in dialogue with the federal government, but made it clear that talks would only proceed if their leader, former Prime Minist...

item.title: African Economies Feel the Impact of Middle East Conflict and Call for Iran Peace Talks
item.description: African countries, including Tanzania, are deeply affected by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly tensions between the US and Iran. While the war hits innocent civilians in the Middl...

item.title: Adebayo Says Tinubu's Leadership Could Not Survive 50 Years Ago
item.description: For Naija matter wey concern our country economy, former SDP presidential candidate, Adewole Adebayo, yan say if President Bola Tinubu dey government 50 years ago, e no go even fit survive, e go dey b...

item.title: Teen Accused of Attempting Plane Hijack at Avalon Airport to Face Mental Health Considerations
item.description: A 19-year-old, who was 17 at the time, has been accused of attempting to hijack a Jetstar flight at Avalon Airport, southwest of Melbourne, in March 2025. The teen allegedly brought a firearm and a fa...

item.title: Alleged Stalker of Lindsey Buckingham Appears in Court Facing Multiple Charges
item.description: Michelle Dick, 55, who is alleged to have stalked and attacked Fleetwood Mac guitarist Lindsey Buckingham, appeared in a Los Angeles court after pleading not guilty to seven criminal charges. She was ...
